title: Operators with Targets
intro: Operators combine with any motion, including line-range and find motions. `d$` deletes to end of line, `dG` to the end of the file, `dtx` deletes up to (but not including) the next `x`, and `dfx` deletes up to and including it.

exercises.instruction: The cursor is on the `=`. Delete from here to the end of the line with `d$`.
hints: `$` is the "end of line" motion; `d$` deletes to it.

exercises.instruction: Delete everything up to (not including) the semicolon with `dt;`.
hints: `t;` is "till the next semicolon" — it stops just before it.

exercises.instruction: Delete the parenthesized call including the closing paren with `df)`.
hints: `f)` finds the next `)`; `df)` deletes through it (unlike `t`, which stops before).

exercises.instruction: Delete from the second line to the end of the file with `dG`.
exercises.initialCode:
keep this line
delete from here
and this
and this too
hints: `G` is the "last line" motion; `dG` deletes from the cursor line to the end.

exercises.instruction: Delete the junk: jump onto the first `j` with `fj`, then `D`.
Delete the key but keep the colon: `dt:`.
hints: `t:` stops just before the colon, so `dt:` leaves it in place.

exercises.instruction: The cursor is on the first `cut` line. Delete everything from here to the end of the file with `dG`.
hints: `dG` is line-wise: it removes the cursor line through the last line.
